/home/learner/README.md:
# Welcome to FictOS Sandbox

This is your personal browser-based Linux environment.
Everything runs in memory — no files are saved to any server.

## Getting started

```bash
ls ~/
cat ~/README.md
nano ~/notes.txt
```

## Available apps

- **Terminal** — full bash-like shell with git support
- **Text Editor** — GUI editor backed by the same filesystem
- **Browser** — sandboxed in-world browser (MDN, Wikipedia)
- **Email** — check your welcome messages

Open the Email app to read your welcome message.
/home/learner/Desktop/getting-started.tx:
GETTING STARTED
===============

1. Open a terminal and try: ls, cd, cat, mkdir, nano
2. Run 'git init' inside any directory to start a git repo
3. Open the Text Editor to create and edit files with a GUI
4. Use the Browser to look up MDN documentation
5. Check Email for your welcome message

Tip: Your files persist for the duration of this browser session.
Refreshing the page reloads from this default state.
# Exercise 1 — Hello World

Run the shell script below:

```bash
cd ~/exercises/01-hello
bash hello.sh
```

Then modify `hello.sh` to print your own message.
# Exercise 2 — Working with Files Practice creating, reading, and organising files: ```bash cd ~/exercises/02-files mkdir my-project touch my-project/index.html echo '

Hello

' > my-project/index.html cat my-project/index.html ```
/home/learner/exercises/03-git/README.md:
# Exercise 3 — Git Basics

Initialise a repository and make your first commit:

```bash
cd ~/exercises/03-git
git init
git status
echo '# My Project' > README.md
git add README.md
git commit -m 'Initial commit'
git log
```
